skill.name: medical-scribe-dictation
skill.description: Convert unstructured physician dictation into professionally formatted SOAP notes with medical terminology normalization and clinical quality validation.
skill.summary: Convert physician verbal dictation into structured SOAP notes with terminology normalization.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 147 patterns across 8 files (4,451 lines), scoring 100/100 risk. All 147 findings were determined to be false positives upon manual evaluation. Markdown code fences were confused with shell backtick execution, medical abbreviations (e.g., 'DES' for diethylstilbestrol) matched cryptographic algorithm names, security checklist items warning against '../' path traversal were flagged as actual vulnerabilities, and medical terminology ('review of systems', 'nervous system') triggered reconnaissance patterns. The skill is a legitimate medical documentation tool with documented opt-in LLM integration and standard file I/O operations. Real concerns are limited to optional LLM API data transmission and file system access, both clearly documented in the skill's own risk assessment.

medium_findings.title: Opt-in Medical Data Transmission to External LLM APIs
medium_findings.description: When the --llm flag is used with 'openai' or 'anthropic' providers, raw medical dictation text is sent to external API endpoints for processing. This is explicitly opt-in via CLI flag and documented in the skill's risk assessment table. The fallback rule-based parser works without any network calls. While the data transmission is intentional and documented, users should be aware that enabling LLM mode sends clinical text to third-party services.

medium_findings.title: File System Read and Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill reads user-provided input files via Path.read_text() and writes output to user-specified paths via Path.write_text(). Path resolution uses Python's pathlib which resolves relative paths but does not enforce sandbox boundaries. The skill's own security checklist correctly documents this and recommends sandboxed execution environments.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Reference Documentation
low_findings.description: The references/terminology-sources.md file contains hardcoded URLs to medical standards organizations (SNOMED, CDC, LOINC, RxNorm). These are legitimate reference links to well-known medical terminology authorities and do not represent data exfiltration. The SKILL.md also contains a GitHub repository URL as a source reference.

low_findings.title: Medical Abbreviation Data File with Common Medical Terms
low_findings.description: The references/medical-abbreviations.json file is a large (2,151 lines) reference dictionary of standard medical abbreviations. Some entries coincidentally match strings used in pattern-based security scanners (e.g., 'DES' which is diethylstilbestrol, not the DES encryption algorithm). The file is static reference data with no executable content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Backtick Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown code block delimiters (` `` `) as Ruby/shell backtick execution across SKILL.md, references/example-cases.md, and references/soap-templates.md. These are standard markdown formatting characters used to display code examples and SOAP note templates in documentation files. No actual shell command injection or dynamic backtick execution exists in these files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Medical Terms Matching Cryptographic Algorithm Names
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged instances of alleged weak cryptographic algorithms across all reference files and scripts. These are medical terms and abbreviations that coincidentally match cryptographic algorithm names: 'DES' (diethylstilbestrol, a synthetic estrogen), 'MD' (medical doctor), and numeric patterns in medication dosages. No cryptographic operations or hashing functions exist in the skill code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security Checklist Items Flagged as Path Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: The SKILL.md security checklist includes items explicitly prohibiting path traversal: 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' at line 213 and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)' at line 216. The static analyzer detected the '../' string in these security rules and incorrectly classified them as actual path traversal attempts rather than preventive security documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Medical Terminology Flagged as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged terms like 'system' across all files as potential system reconnaissance. In context, these are standard medical phrases: 'review of systems' (a medical examination component), 'nervous system', 'immune system', 'operating system' (in technical dependency notes), and 'EHR systems'. These are medical and technical documentation terms with no reconnaissance intent.

content.user_title: Convert Medical Dictation into Structured SOAP Notes
content.value_statement: Physicians and medical scribes lose hours formatting unstructured clinical dictation into formal notes. This skill automates SOAP note generation from dictation with medical terminology normalization, vital sign extraction, and clinical completeness validation.
actual_capabilities: Convert unstructured physician dictation into structured SOAP notes with all four standard sections
Normalize medical abbreviations and expand clinical acronyms using a built-in terminology database
Extract vital signs, medications, and diagnoses from free-text dictation using regex and optional LLM parsing
Generate specialty-specific clinical documentation templates for eight medical specialties
Validate clinical note completeness and flag missing required elements with confidence scoring
Process both pre-transcribed text input and audio files through optional Whisper integration
limitations: Medical terminology accuracy depends on dictation clarity and speech quality
All generated notes require physician review before entering any medical record system
The skill structures clinical information but does not provide diagnostic suggestions or treatment recommendations
Drug name identification uses pattern matching and should be verified by the prescribing physician

use_cases.title: Clinical Documentation for Physicians
use_cases.description: A primary care physician dictates patient encounter notes after each visit and needs them converted to structured SOAP format for EHR entry, saving documentation time while maintaining clinical accuracy.

use_cases.title: Medical Scribe Training and Education
use_cases.description: Medical scribe trainees practice with example dictations to learn proper SOAP note structure, comparing their manually written notes against AI-generated templates for skill development.

use_cases.title: Academic Medical Writing Support
use_cases.description: Medical researchers and students convert clinical case descriptions into standardized documentation format for case reports, academic papers, and clinical presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SOAP Note from Dictation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this physician dictation into a structured SOAP note with Subjective, Objective, Assessment, and Plan sections: [paste dictation text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: A physician has a short dictation from a routine follow-up visit and needs it formatted quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Specialty-Specific Clinical Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the internal medicine template, process this consultation dictation into a SOAP note. Extract and normalize all vital signs, list current medications, and identify the primary diagnosis with clinical reasoning: [paste dictation text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: An internist needs a comprehensive note from a detailed consultation dictation with medication reconciliation.

prompt_templates.title: Emergency Department Note with Red Flags
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this emergency department dictation into a SOAP note using the emergency medicine template. Flag any abnormal vital signs, identify time-critical findings, and note any missing required elements for acute care documentation: [paste dictation text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: An ED physician dictates a complex acute case and needs a structured note highlighting critical findings.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Specialty Handoff with Full Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this multi-specialty patient handoff dictation into a comprehensive SOAP note. Normalize all medical abbreviations against standard terminology, extract and cross-reference medications, generate differential diagnoses, validate completeness against CMS documentation guidelines, and flag any ambiguous or conflicting clinical information: [paste dictation text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: A hospitalist receives a complex patient handoff requiring thorough documentation for continuity of care.

output_examples.input: Patient is a 58-year-old male here for follow-up of hypertension. Reports taking lisinopril 10mg daily but misses doses occasionally. BP 148/92, HR 78. Denies chest pain or shortness of breath. Continues to smoke half pack per day.
output_examples.output: A structured clinical note with Chief Complaint identifying hypertension follow-up, History of Present Illness documenting medication adherence and symptom review, Vital Signs table showing BP 148/92 mmHg and HR 78 bpm, Social History noting active tobacco use, Assessment diagnosing uncontrolled hypertension, and Plan recommending lisinopril dose increase with smoking cessation counseling and 4-week follow-up.

output_examples.input: Six-month-old male for well child check. Full term, birth weight 7lbs 8oz. Taking 6oz formula every 4 hours, started solids last month. Rolling over, sitting with support, smiling. Weight 17lbs 4oz (75th percentile), length 26 inches (50th percentile).
output_examples.output: A pediatric well-child SOAP note with growth parameters including percentile rankings, developmental milestone checklist showing age-appropriate progress (rolling, sitting, social smiling), feeding and nutrition history, immunization status tracking, normal physical examination findings by system, and anticipatory guidance covering solid food introduction and safety counseling.

output_examples.input: 45-year-old female to ED with chest pain onset 2 hours ago at rest. Pressure-like, 8/10, radiating to left arm. Nausea and diaphoresis present. ECG shows ST elevation V2-V4. Troponin 0.5 trending up. Given aspirin 325mg, ticagrelor load, heparin bolus.
output_examples.output: An emergency medicine SOAP note with time-stamped symptom onset and pain characteristics, cardiac risk factor assessment, vital signs and physical examination findings, diagnostic results including ECG interpretation and elevated cardiac biomarkers, primary STEMI diagnosis with ESI level 1 acuity rating, and complete intervention documentation listing all administered medications with doses and cath lab activation status.

best_practices: Always review generated SOAP notes for clinical accuracy and completeness before adding to any medical record system
Provide complete dictation including chief complaint, history, examination findings, and assessment for the highest quality structured output
Specify the medical specialty when processing dictation to use the appropriate template with relevant fields and terminology
anti_patterns: Using AI-generated clinical notes in patient records without thorough physician review and explicit approval
Expecting the skill to provide diagnostic suggestions, treatment recommendations, or clinical decision support beyond documentation structuring
Submitting dictation with ambiguous or non-standard abbreviations without providing sufficient clinical context for accurate disambiguation

faq.question: What types of medical dictation does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill processes any medical dictation containing clinical information such as chief complaint, history of present illness, physical examination findings, assessment, and treatment plan.

faq.question: Can this skill process audio recordings directly?
faq.answer: Yes, when the optional Whisper dependency is installed. The skill can transcribe audio files in WAV, MP3, or M4A format before generating the SOAP note.

faq.question: Do I need an LLM provider for this skill to work?
faq.answer: No. The skill includes a rule-based parser that works without any external API. LLM integration with OpenAI or Anthropic is optional and can be enabled for improved extraction accuracy.

faq.question: Can the generated notes be used directly in electronic health records?
faq.answer: No. All generated notes must be reviewed and approved by the attending physician before entering any medical record system. The skill includes a disclaimer in every output.

faq.question: Which medical specialties does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill includes templates for eight specialties: internal medicine, emergency medicine, cardiology, pediatrics, surgery, psychiatry, OB/GYN, and orthopedics.

faq.question: What languages does the skill support for dictation?
faq.answer: The skill primarily supports English medical dictation. The audio transcription feature includes a language configuration option for additional languages.
